creator_thesis: @kai_rehagen is a visual-first creator who treats images as autonomous art objects, deploying radical caption minimalism — blank text, deadpan generic titles, or a single emoji — as a deliberate refusal of platform content conventions and algorithmic legibility.

key_concepts.name: Radical caption minimalism
key_concepts.definition: The practice of posting with no caption, a single glyph, or a near-empty label as an aesthetic stance rather than oversight.

key_concepts.name: Photography as art object
key_concepts.definition: Images positioned as self-contained objects that carry full meaning without textual mediation, aligning them with gallery work rather than social content.

key_concepts.name: Silence as aesthetic choice
key_concepts.definition: The deliberate absence of language — blank captions, withheld context — used as a compositional element signaling refusal of explanation.

key_concepts.name: Anti-algorithm posting
key_concepts.definition: A posting strategy that omits keywords, hooks, hashtags, and discoverability cues, treating algorithmic indifference as a value rather than a liability.

key_concepts.name: Vernacular titling / deadpan irony
key_concepts.definition: Using the most generic possible label ('Some Pictures') as an ironic negation of branding, echoing the flat titling conventions of photobooks and exhibition catalogs.

key_concepts.name: Emoji as total caption
key_concepts.definition: Reducing the entire communicative gesture of a caption to a single glyph, preserving affect while stripping all descriptive or contextual language.

key_concepts.name: Platform refusal
key_concepts.definition: A conscious rejection of social media content conventions — no narrative, no call-to-action, no hook — that repositions the feed as exhibition space.

positions: Images require no textual mediation — a caption explains what an art object should resist explaining.
'Some Pictures' is the most generic title imaginable, and that genericity is the point: ironic deflation of content branding.
A single flower emoji (💐) contains more affective precision than any descriptive caption could.
Refusing algorithmic hooks is a political gesture — illegibility to the platform is a form of integrity.
Silence and negative space are not absences but active compositional choices that position the work as art, not content.
